mnemonics: CPU Time = IC x CPI x Clock Period (PIC a Perfect Clock for Performance)
Think P.I.C. for Performance: Program (IC), Instructions (CPI), Clock
keyPoints: CPU Time = (Instruction Count) x (Cycles Per Instruction) x (Clock Cycle Time)
Performance is inversely proportional to execution time; to improve performance, you must decrease execution time.
The equation highlights the three main factors that affect processor performance: the program, the instruction set architecture, and the hardware implementation.
commonMistakes: Confusing CPI (Cycles Per Instruction) with IPC (Instructions Per Cycle); they are inverses (CPI = 1/IPC).
Forgetting that the equation must use consistent units, e.g., clock cycle time in seconds, not frequency in Hz (Time = 1/Frequency).
